summary: Expand backend fixes to include `check_uplink` improvements (stable `botIdIndex`, indexed lookup, non-empty signature + TODO for Ed25519 verification) and update `fetchSignals` in `backend/main.mo` to return structured HTTP outcall response data (statusCode/body/timestamp), add an audit entry with first 100 chars of the response, and update public types—without modifying other endpoints.
architectural_notes: Stripe integration must not modify backend/stripe/stripe.mo; actor should store optional stable StripeConfiguration and call Stripe.createCheckoutSession/getSessionStatus using existing transform function.
Expose Stripe-related types (ShoppingItem, StripeSessionStatus) as public types from the actor for frontend consumption.
Project context: "Smart Money Vault" (institutional trading command center on ICP) with single Motoko canister backend/main.mo + React frontend + off-chain Python trading bot.

Constraints: preserve ALL legacy public methods listed in backend/main.mo header; all mutations must be update calls (never query); keep files <1000 lines (split into modules if needed); do not modify stable/tested modules unless explicitly told; do not modify frontend paths: frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui/*.
Access control rules: use AccessControl.hasPermission for user checks and AccessControl.isAdmin for admin checks.

Cycle balance API constraint: Runtime.cyclesBalance() is not available in the current Motoko version; use ExperimentalCycles.balance() if available, otherwise set cycles to 0 and add comment `// TODO: Replace with actual cycle balance API when available.`

For `check_uplink` performance and correctness, maintain a stable `botIdIndex` (bot_id → owner Principal) updated in `saveCallerUserProfile`, and use it instead of scanning all `userProfiles`. Enforce non-empty signature with a trap and include a Phase 2 TODO for full Ed25519 verification.
Connect Bot Wizard must generate a real Ed25519 keypair (TweetNaCl preferred) so the registered publicKey corresponds to the user’s private key; avoid unrelated random byte arrays.
known_issues: Repeated build attempts for the getHeartbeatData change failed with the assistant reporting it 'couldn't implement that request'; root cause not identified in conversation.
Motoko version lacks Runtime.cyclesBalance(); heartbeat cycles must use ExperimentalCycles.balance() or fall back to 0 with a TODO comment.
Attempt to implement the `fetchSignals` structured HTTP response return and type change failed (assistant reported inability to implement); retry requested with smaller substeps.

existing_features.title: Update the dashboard heartbeat UI to tolerate and display the new optional `cyclesWarning` field returned by the backend heartbeat API.

Update the frontend connectivity-test hook `frontend/src/hooks/useFetchSignalsTest.ts` to handle the new `fetchSignals` success shape (`{ statusCode; body; timestamp }`) and update the success toast message to reflect the structured result (e.g., include status code and/or body length) rather than `signals.length`.

Regenerate or update the frontend’s backend interface type definitions (e.g., the generated declarations used by the actor) so TypeScript correctly reflects the updated `SignalFetchResult` Candid type and `actor.fetchSignals()` return type.

Update `frontend/src/components/settings/ConnectBotWizardModal.tsx` so the Connect Bot Wizard generates a real Ed25519 keypair (where the public key matches the private key) using TweetNaCl (`tweetnacl`) if available; if TweetNaCl is not available, use `@noble/ed25519` instead, and if dependency installation is not possible, fall back to Web Crypto API Ed25519 key generation.

Replace the existing `handleGenerateKeypair` implementation in `frontend/src/components/settings/ConnectBotWizardModal.tsx` with the Ed25519 key generation flow specified by the user: generate the keypair, display the private key as a hex string (BOT_PRIVATE_KEY), and keep the public key as a `Uint8Array` for sending to the backend; keep all other wizard steps, UI behaviors (blur/copy), and the existing `linkMutation` call flow unchanged.

new_feature_requests.summary: Wire existing backend/stripe/stripe.mo module into actor public interface: add stable stripeConfig, admin setStripeConfig, query isStripeConfigured, user-auth createCheckoutSession/update and getStripeSessionStatus/query; re-export ShoppingItem and StripeSessionStatus types; do not modify stripe.mo or existing endpoints/types.

Wire the existing Stripe module into the backend actor public interface without modifying the Stripe module file. Import the Stripe module from `backend/stripe/stripe.mo` and use its existing `createCheckoutSession` and `getSessionStatus` functions from the actor.

Add stable Stripe configuration storage and admin/user-gated Stripe endpoints to `backend/main.mo`:
- Add stable variable `stripeConfig : ?StripeConfiguration` (type imported from the Stripe module).
- Add `isStripeConfigured` (query): returns `Bool` indicating whether `stripeConfig` is non-null.
- Add `setStripeConfig` (update, admin only): input `secretKey : Text`, `allowedCountries : [Text]`; store as `StripeConfiguration`; return `#ok("Configured")`.
- Add `createCheckoutSession` (update, authenticated user required): input `items : [ShoppingItem]`, `successUrl : Text`, `cancelUrl : Text`; if `stripeConfig` is null return `#err` with message `"Stripe not configured"`; otherwise call `Stripe.createCheckoutSession` with (config, caller, items, URLs, and the existing actor `transform` function) and return the resulting `Text` (JSON string).
- Add `getStripeSessionStatus` (query, authenticated user required): input `sessionId : Text`; if `stripeConfig` is null return an error; otherwise call `Stripe.getSessionStatus` with (config, sessionId, and the existing actor `transform` function) and return the `StripeSessionStatus` result.

Import `ShoppingItem` and `StripeSessionStatus` types from `backend/stripe/stripe.mo` and re-export them as public actor types so the frontend can use them. Ensure any necessary upgrade/migration logic preserves existing stable state and initializes the newly added `stripeConfig` stable field safely during upgrades.

Fix legacy getHeartbeatData in backend/main.mo: replace hardcoded values by looking up caller BotProfile (botProfiles) and UserProfile (license info), compute cycles using Runtime.cyclesBalance() or ExperimentalCycles.balance() (else 0 with TODO), set botStatus (#ACTIVE if botProfile exists and uplinkStatus true else #SUSPENDED), set lastHeartbeatAt (botProfile.lastHeartbeat else 0), set verifiedLicense by checking active license in licenses map via user accountId, and add backward-compatible optional field cyclesWarning:?Text when cycles < 500_000_000_000; do not modify other endpoints.

Fix the legacy `getHeartbeatData` implementation in `backend/main.mo` to return real data instead of hardcoded placeholders, while keeping the same public method name and overall single-actor architecture intact.
Extend the backend `HeartbeatData` type to include a backward-compatible optional `cyclesWarning : ?Text` field and populate it when the canister cycle balance is below 500_000_000_000.

Wire the existing Stripe module (`backend/stripe/stripe.mo`) into the main actor public interface in `backend/main.mo` without modifying `backend/stripe/stripe.mo`, including stable storage for Stripe configuration and the required admin/user-gated endpoints.

Ensure upgrades preserve existing canister state while safely introducing the new stable `stripeConfig` field (adding conditional migration logic only if required by the current project’s upgrade policy).

Update `backend/main.mo` `getHeartbeatData` so the `cycles` value no longer uses any unavailable cycles balance API: attempt to use `ExperimentalCycles.balance()` for the canister cycle balance; if `ExperimentalCycles.balance()` is not available in this Motoko version, set `cycles` to `0` and add the exact comment `// TODO: Replace with actual cycle balance API when available.`. Do not change any other logic, types, fields, access control, or behavior in `getHeartbeatData` beyond this `cycles` retrieval adjustment.

Implement `check_uplink` fixes in `backend/main.mo`:
- Add stable `botIdIndex : Map<Text, Principal>` mapping `bot_id` to owner Principal.
- Update `saveCallerUserProfile` to maintain `botIdIndex` after storing the profile: if new `bot_id` exists, remove old mapping when `bot_id` changed, then add `newBotId -> caller`.
- Replace `check_uplink` to use `botIdIndex.get(bot_id)` (trap with `"Unknown bot ID: no user registered with this bot_id"` if null), then fetch `BotProfile` (trap `"No bot profile found for this bot ID"` if null), require `publicKey` (trap `"Bot public key not registered. Complete the Connect Bot wizard first."` if null), validate `signature.size() > 0` else trap `"Missing signature: signature blob cannot be empty. The Python bot must sign requests with its private key."`, add TODO `// TODO Phase 2: Implement full Ed25519 signature verification`, and return `#EXECUTE` if `uplinkStatus` else `#STANDBY`.
- Do not modify any other endpoints.
In `backend/main.mo`, add a new stable map `botIdIndex : Map<Text, Principal>` that maps `bot_id` (Text) to the owning user Principal.

In `backend/main.mo` `saveCallerUserProfile`, after storing the incoming profile, update `botIdIndex` when `profile.bot_id` is non-null: (1) if a previous profile existed for the caller and had a different `bot_id`, remove the old `bot_id` entry from `botIdIndex`; (2) add/update the mapping `new bot_id -> caller` in `botIdIndex`.

Replace the `check_uplink` implementation in `backend/main.mo` to: (1) resolve the owner Principal via `botIdIndex.get(bot_id)`; if null, `Runtime.trap("Unknown bot ID: no user registered with this bot_id")`; (2) fetch the owner’s `BotProfile`; if null, `Runtime.trap("No bot profile found for this bot ID")`; (3) require `publicKey` exists; if null, `Runtime.trap("Bot public key not registered. Complete the Connect Bot wizard first.")`; (4) validate `signature` is non-empty; if `signature.size() == 0`, `Runtime.trap("Missing signature: signature blob cannot be empty. The Python bot must sign requests with its private key.")`; (5) add the comment `// TODO Phase 2: Implement full Ed25519 signature verification`; (6) return `#EXECUTE` if `uplinkStatus` is true, else `#STANDBY`.

Fix `fetchSignals` in `backend/main.mo` to return the HTTP response body instead of `#ok([])`: change `SignalFetchResult` to `#ok : { statusCode : Nat; body : Text; timestamp : Int } | #err : BotError`. On successful outcall (no trap), return `#ok({ statusCode = 200; body = response; timestamp = Time.now() })` while still updating `lastHeartbeat` as currently implemented; keep existing `#err` returns on errors. Add an audit entry: `"Signals fetched: " # (if response.size() > 100 then first 100 chars else full response)`. Update public type definitions so frontend can consume the new result shape. Do not modify any other endpoints.

Update the backend public type `SignalFetchResult` in `backend/main.mo` to return a structured HTTP response payload instead of `[Signal]`, changing it to: `#ok : { statusCode : Nat; body : Text; timestamp : Int } | #err : BotError` (keeping the existing `BotError` variants unchanged).

In `backend/main.mo`, update `fetchSignals` so that after a successful HTTP outcall (i.e., after `response = await OutCall.httpGetRequest(...)` completes without trapping), the function returns `#ok({ statusCode = 200; body = response; timestamp = Time.now() })` instead of `#ok([])`.

Keep the existing `fetchSignals` error behavior in `backend/main.mo` unchanged: on authorization failure, missing bot profile, missing bot URL, invalid URL, or any existing error case, continue returning the same `#err` variants and messages as currently implemented.

In `backend/main.mo`, ensure `fetchSignals` still updates the caller’s bot profile heartbeat timestamp exactly as it currently does (i.e., set `lastHeartbeat = Time.now()` in the stored `BotProfile`) on the successful outcall path.

In `backend/main.mo`, add an audit log entry on the successful outcall path of `fetchSignals` whose details include the first 100 characters of the HTTP response body (or the full body if it is <= 100 characters), using the exact prefix format: `"Signals fetched: " # <snippet>`.

Regenerate the frontend Candid-derived TypeScript interface/declaration files so the generated `SignalFetchResult` and `actor.fetchSignals()` types match the updated backend result shape (`{ ok: { statusCode; body; timestamp } } | { err: BotError }`).

Regenerate or update the frontend’s Candid-derived TypeScript interface/declaration files used by the frontend actor (the `backendInterface` imported from `../backend`) so TypeScript correctly reflects the updated backend `SignalFetchResult` shape returned by `actor.fetchSignals()` (success payload includes `statusCode`, `body`, and `timestamp`).

Fix Ed25519 key generation in Connect Bot Wizard (frontend/src/components/settings/ConnectBotWizardModal.tsx): replace unrelated random buffers with a real Ed25519 keypair using TweetNaCl (preferred) or fallback to @noble/ed25519 / WebCrypto Ed25519 if dependency install isn’t possible; display secretKey as hex (BOT_PRIVATE_KEY) and keep publicKey as Uint8Array for backend; do not modify any other files/components.

If TweetNaCl is not currently present in the project dependencies, add it as a frontend dependency so `import nacl from 'tweetnacl'` compiles and runs; do not introduce any other dependency changes beyond what is required to support the Ed25519 keypair generation fix.

projectName: Expose Stripe checkout/session APIs via backend actor (stable config, admin setup, user-auth calls)
